description_long: This is a traditional stone water well with a cylindrical brick base and a pitched roof made of blue wooden planks. The well features a wooden support frame holding up the roof, with a visible crank mechanism and a rope suspending a wooden bucket over the open shaft. The stonework of the well is composed of dark and light bricks, giving it a robust and weathered appearance. The roof is supported by thick wooden beams, and the blue color of the roof contrasts with the earthy tones of the stone and wood. The bucket and crank are centrally positioned, indicating the well's primary function for drawing water. There are no visible decorative elements, and the structure is utilitarian in design.
description: A stone water well with a blue wooden roof, wooden support beams, and a central bucket and crank mechanism. The design is sturdy and functional, with contrasting blue and earthy tones.
description_view_0: Front view showing the blue roof, wooden support beams, and the bucket hanging over the well opening.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the triangular profile of the roof, the wooden frame, and the crank mechanism.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, with the blue roof and bucket visible, but less detail on the crank.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the wooden frame structure and the crank wheel more clearly.
attribute_well_cover_design: No cover; open shaft beneath a pitched blue wooden roof supported by wooden beams.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: A wooden bucket is suspended by a rope, operated by a visible crank mechanism on the side.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: Cylindrical stone wall approximately waist-high encircling the well opening, with a wooden roof supported by beams.
